title: Desborough Fortress Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed including Flt Sgt. Hubert Charles Gerard Brook

external_narratives.summary: Memorial record for the crew of Boeing Fortress Mk.I AN534 WP-E of 90 Squadron. The aircraft suffered catastrophic structural failure during a high-altitude test flight on 28 July 1941, with the starboard wing breaking off in severe turbulence.
key_details: Starboard wing structural failure in severe turbulence
Departed Polebrook at 17:00 for high altitude test
Crashed 4 miles west of Corby near Stoke Albany

external_narratives.summary: RAF Commands forum discussion with historical details of Flying Fortress AN534 from 90 Squadron. Documents the aircraft's history as one of the early RAF-operated B-17s based at Polebrook.
key_details: One of earliest RAF B-17 operations

external_narratives.summary: Reference entry on 90 Squadron's operation of Boeing Fortress aircraft. Documents the squadron's use of early B-17C models received under Lend-Lease, including AN534.

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep
